input.background: A manufacturer has observed that over the course of 10 months, the numbers of products demanded were $T_1, T_2, \ldots, T_{10}$, respectively. If we assume that these are i.i.d. observations of demand.
input.question: What does it mean for $X_n$ to converge with probability 1 (or almost surely) to $X$?

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 1: Basic Probability, Statistics, and Reliability
